headline: 警方西貢反爆竊巡邏行動拘捕兩名外籍男子
excerpt:
警方東九龍總區警察機動部隊人員在西貢村屋一帶展開反爆竊巡邏行動，今早約9時，在壁屋村路發現兩名形跡可疑男子，上前截查後在他們身上檢獲多種可用於偷竊的物品，包括螺絲批、手套及鐵桿等，以涉嫌「外出時備有偷竊用的物品」，拘捕這兩名分別39歲及50歲的外籍男子。

初步調查顯示，他們企圖於西貢區的獨立屋內進行盜竊，案件已交由將軍澳警區刑事調查隊第一隊跟進。

警方表示，因應復活節長假期將至，市民外遊及外出活動增加，入屋爆竊風險相對提升，提醒市民應加緊看管可放置財物的地點，防止不法分子有機可乘，又重申對所有犯罪行為採取零容忍態度，會繼續進行反爆竊行動及巡邏。



編輯：陳樂謙
summary_zh: 警方東九龍總區在西貢展開反爆竊巡邏行動，拘捕兩名持有偷竊工具的外籍男子，初步調查顯示他們企圖在西貢獨立屋進行盜竊。

headline: 醫管局九龍東聯網逾5.6萬名病人資料外洩　疑有人盜取資料
excerpt:
醫管局發現一宗懷疑未經授權將病人資料取走，並於第三方平台洩漏的個案，涉及九龍東醫院聯網逾5.6萬名病人的資料，包括姓名、身份證號碼、醫院檔案號碼和手術內容等。

醫管局指，事件不涉及網絡攻擊等因素。醫管局行政總裁李夏茵向員工發信，指事件懷疑與有人非法盜取病人資料有關，外洩的資料亦包括一個聯網少量的員工姓名及職級。

警方表示，案件已交由網絡安全及科技罪案調查科跟進。



編輯：梁家奇
summary_zh: 醫管局九龍東聯網發現超過5.6萬名病人資料遭人盜取並在第三方平台洩漏，涉及姓名、身份證號碼等敏感資訊，警方已介入調查。

headline: 羅淑佩：中東局勢無損旅客來港意欲
excerpt:
內地一連三日的清明節假期展開，陸續有內地旅客來港。文化體育及旅遊局局長羅淑佩表示，對4月旅客訪港的情況感樂觀，預計今年「五一黃金周」將有100萬人次旅客訪港。她又認為，現時的中東局勢，不會影響旅客來港意欲。



編輯：梁家奇
summary_zh: 旅遊局局長羅淑佩表示對4月旅客訪港情況樂觀，預計「五一黃金周」將有100萬人次訪港，認為中東局勢不會影響旅客來港意欲。

headline: 李家超：大力推動更多課後託管服務
excerpt:
行政長官李家超到訪九龍城區一間有參與「在校課後託管服務計劃」的小學。他表示，樂見計劃有成效，會與團隊研究進一步擴展計劃，亦會大力推動更多類似的課後託管服務。



編輯：梁家奇
summary_zh: 行政長官李家超視察參與課後託管服務計劃的小學，表示將大力推動擴展更多類似服務。

headline: 「兩蚊兩折」首日九巴回贈機數額不準　政府指公帑及乘客沒損失
excerpt:
「二元優惠」乘車計劃昨日起調整為「兩蚊兩折」，有報道指，九巴有車費回贈機懷疑故障，回贈了全額車費。九巴指屬個別拍卡機問題，目前已經處理，確認現時系統及拍卡機運作正常；又指根據資料顯示，沒有乘客因此多付車費，亦不會令政府有額外補貼金額。 

勞工及福利局局長孫玉菡表示，運輸署已進行測試，並要求九巴調查及清楚提交報告，重申所有錯誤數額由九巴承擔，公帑及乘客都沒有任何損失。 



編輯：梁家奇
summary_zh: 「兩蚊兩折」優惠計劃首日，九巴部分回贈機故障導致金額計算不準，惟政府表示公帑及乘客均無損失，所有責任由九巴承擔。

編輯：黃佩珊
summary_zh: 清明節假期首日，截至下午4時共有逾72萬人次進出香港，其中入境逾24萬人次、出境逾48萬人次。

headline: 兩男子涉及爆竊東涌食肆被捕　警方尋回兩架失車起回部分贓物
excerpt:
東涌馬灣涌一間食肆上月懷疑被爆竊，警方昨日拘捕兩名男子，涉嫌「爆竊」及「擅自取去交通工具」，兩人正被扣查。

警方上月24日接報，相關食肆有約2000元現金、兩把私家車後備車匙、一個公司印章、兩張未兌現支票及兩本收據簿被盜去，食肆負責人其後亦發現兩輛私家車被偷。

警方翻查大量閉路電視，包括「銳眼計劃」下安裝的閉路電視，經調查及分析，前日在落馬洲區尋回兩輛失車，警員昨日再在上水區拘捕兩名分別37歲及29歲本地男子。警方行動中起回部分失物包括兩把車匙、行車證及兩本收據簿。



編輯：陳樂謙
summary_zh: 警方拘捕兩名男子涉嫌爆竊東涌食肆及偷車，已尋回兩架失車及部分贓物。

headline: 警方拘9名男子涉多項罪行　其中一人被搜出市值逾436萬元毒品
excerpt:
警方日前展開反三合會行動，拘捕9名本地男子，涉嫌非法禁錮、販毒、刑事毀壞、傷人等，其中一人被暫控販毒。

警方本周四至五共巡查超過70個娛樂場所，並利用小型無人機巡邏，期間在大窩口村附近一個公園截查一名50歲男子時，在他的背囊及褲袋中檢獲10塊磚頭形狀的懷疑可卡因毒品，約重5.12公斤，市值超過436萬元。

警方相信有販毒集團以金錢利誘，招募想賺快錢的人士販運毒品，相信今次行動已成功打擊販毒集團，並阻止一大批毒品流入市面。

被捕人士年齡介乎23至71歲，部分人有黑社會背景，或涉及黑社會相關的案件。



編輯：陳樂謙
summary_zh: 警方展開反三合會行動拘捕9名男子，涉及非法禁錮、販毒等多項罪行，其中一人被搜出市值436萬元的可卡因毒品。

headline: 李夏茵向員工發信　病人資料外洩疑與有人非法盜取資料有關
excerpt:
因應醫管局病人資料外洩資料事件，醫管局行政總裁李夏茵向員工發信，強調重視病人資料安全，並已採取多項措施加強醫療系統以防止資料外洩。經初步檢視，網絡系統運作正常，暫時無證據顯示涉及網絡攻擊，事件疑與有人非法盜取病人資料有關。醫管局會全力配合警方調查外洩原因。
 
醫管局的恆常監察系統於4月3日凌晨約2時，發現有公立醫院病人資料外洩，並不當地上載至其他平台。外洩資料以電腦原始檔案形式顯示，包含病人姓名、性別、身份證號碼、醫院檔案號碼及手術內容等資料。資料內亦包括一個聯網少量的員工姓名及職級，但未有發現員工身份證號碼或其他個人資料包含在內。
 
醫管局高度重視並嚴肅處理此事，已即時報警及通報個人資料私隱專員公署，並要求相關第三方平台移除所有外洩資料。
 


編輯：陳樂謙
summary_zh: 醫管局病人資料外洩事件，行政總裁李夏茵向員工發信強調重視資料安全，初步查明事件與非法盜取有關而非網絡攻擊，將全力配合警方調查。

headline: 中國駐以色列大使館提醒中國公民加強安全防範並及時轉移避險
excerpt:
中國駐以色列大使館提醒在當地的中國公民，加強安全防範，遠離民用基礎設施，及時轉移避險。

提示指當前中東安全形勢高度緊張，戰事擴大升級的風險仍然極高，並可能危及學校、電站、工業園區等各類民用機構。

使館說將於4月7日再組織一批在以中國公民，包括港澳台同胞，通過埃及塔巴口岸轉移撤離，在以同胞特別是身處高風險地區的同胞，可透過使館渠道登記個人信息，並做好有關準備。



編輯：羅宇光
summary_zh: 中國駐以色列大使館因中東安全形勢緊張，提醒中國公民加強防範並及時避險，將於4月7日組織新一批撤離。

headline: 蘇州科企盼加強與香港技術互通與人才交流
excerpt:
江蘇蘇州近年積極推動「具身智能」等創科產業，並協助企業對接潛在客戶。有蘇州的科技園負責人表示，香港是科技與人才高地，未來會加強兩地技術互通和人才交流。



編輯：梁家奇
summary_zh: 蘇州科技企業表示將加強與香港的技術合作與人才交流，以推動創科產業發展。

headline: 內地清明假期展開　全社會今日跨區域人員流動量近3億人次
excerpt:
內地一連三天清明節假期展開。交通運輸部預計，今天全社會跨區域人員流動量接近3億人次，同比增長2.7%。

交通運輸部表示，其中，公路人員流動量最多，預計超過2億7千萬人次。鐵路客運量預計超過2100萬人次，水路客運量則預計約135萬人次。

至於民航客運量，預計同比下降13.4%，約為192萬人次。



編輯：羅宇光
summary_zh: 內地清明假期展開，全社會今日跨區域人員流動量接近3億人次，同比增長2.7%，其中公路流動量最多，民航客運量則下降。

headline: Buffett may end donations to Gates charity over Bill's ties to Epstein
excerpt: Revelations about Jeffrey Epstein's ties to Bill Gates have strained his friendship with Warren Buffett and the Omaha billionaire's annual multi-billion-dollar donations to the Gates Foundation.
full_text:
BECKY QUICK: Warren, welcome. It is wonderful to see you this morning.

WARREN BUFFETT: It is fun to be on.

QUICK: You are on for an interesting reason.

For 22 years, you had been holding an annual luncheon — an auction for a luncheon — to benefit the Glide Foundation in San Francisco.

You retired from that – from doing that — back in 2022 after you'd raised more than 50 million dollars. I think the last auction that you raised — that you did — raised – 19 million —

BUFFETT: 19 million.

QUICK: — one hundred dollars [$19,000,100] for the Glide Foundation.

And you kind of hung it up and said that was going to be the end.

You are back with a new announcement today that there is a new auction that is coming, with a twist.

This time it's Warren Buffett, Stephen Curry and Ayesha Curry, and they're going to be having a new luncheon to benefit not only the Glide Foundation, but the Currys' foundation as well, which is, Eat, Learn, Play.

And this auction is going to be held May 7th. It starts at 7:30pm Pacific time. It closes on May 14 at 7:30pm exactly.

And all of the benefits of that is going to go to benefit these two foundations, Glide and the Eat, Learn, Play Foundation.

How did this come about? Why did you unretire from this?

BUFFETT: Well, let me tell you first how I got into it, because my first wife, Susie, was living in San Francisco and she said to me, this guy is real. (Laughs) And — and —

QUICK: This guy being Cecil.

BUFFETT: Cecil. Yeah, Cecil Williams, who came to that church in 1963. And it was a dying church in a changing part of the neighborhood in San Francisco. And they weren't glad to see him, the hundred or so parishioners that were left.

But he turned it into something that became — it gave hope and life to people that the world had given up on.

And I went on Sunday still expecting something less than that — (laughs) — and I watched Cecil, and I could see what he was doing, and he was for real.

And so, Susie, at some point, said, why don't you do something to raise some money for him? You know, and so, I think she actually selected the idea of the lunch, and then we did the lunch.

The first three lunches brought 25 thousand dollars each because they were localized. And then we got the idea of going on eBay. And then we started getting bids from around the world.

And it just generally kept moving up, although it wasn't every single year, but it just — it just put us on the map.

And as the final amount, 19 million, was raised — now that was kind of raised because it was the last one, I think. I was doing (inaudible) that had bought an earlier lunch, but I didn't make any calls to him or do anything. He just turned out to be — it inspires people. And Smith & Wollensky, as you know, covered it in New York sometimes.

QUICK: That's often where you had the lunch with the winners of this.

BUFFETT: And some of them wanted to be anonymous. And a couple came to Omaha along the line because they had some special thing they wanted to talk about.

But I think everybody felt like they were glad they did it, and I was glad to do it, and —

QUICK: So why'd you stop?

BUFFETT: Well, I ran out of gas. (Laughs)

I — you know, I got to be what, 93 at that time, or something like that. And it just — same reason I gave up teaching.

I teach — I was — I taught every year from when I was 21 till 88 or 89. And there just came a period when your body said different things to you. And you should turn it over to somebody, just like I did at Berkshire.

I mean, at different times, on different things, but I — and so we thought we had a continuation of it all set up, and then for one reason or another, it fizzled.

And so the last two years they — well, I think the first year, some board member made up some members — but basically the auction disappeared.

QUICK: So they haven't had the funds coming in.

BUFFETT: No. And Cecil Williams was about my age in all this — and it got so I couldn't understand him on the phone or anything like that, but all he wanted was this to continue.

And so, I don't know where the idea came from exactly, but I said I would do one more, just to get us started again and to have Steph Curry join us, I mean, in the Bay Area. I mean, it's just a natural. And —

QUICK: Right. The Eat, Learn, Play Foundation that Stephen and Ayesha Curry have set up is in the Bay Area as well, so they're —

BUFFETT: Exactly.

QUICK: — locally focused on all of these things.

For people who don't know, the Glide Foundation was also, it was highlighted in the movie "The Pursuit of Happyness" with Will Smith. So people may have a little bit of an idea of what that's about.

Why Steph Curry? How did that come about?

BUFFETT: Well, I mean, who can say it better?

I mean, he's worked with the kids in Oakland. I mean, these are kids between five and 15, or something like that, and he plays basketball with them.

And I mean, he's a terrific guy. I don't — you know, I haven't met him personally.

QUICK:...
summary_zh: 巴菲特因蓋茨與愛潑斯坦的關係而考慮終止對蓋茨基金會的巨額捐款，兩人的友誼因此受到影響。

headline: 截至晚上9時　本港約108萬人次出入境
excerpt:
内地一連3日清明節假期展開，入境處表示，截至晚上9時，有接近108萬人次進出香港，其中入境佔超過42萬4千人次，最多人經落馬洲管制站入境，其次是羅湖口岸。入境的內地旅客達12萬8千人次。

至於出境方面，錄得超過65萬5千人次，本港居民達53萬2千人次。最多人經羅湖口岸出境，其次是落馬洲管制站。



編輯：陳樂謙
summary_zh: 清明節假期首日，香港錄得約108萬人次出入境，當中入境逾42萬人次、出境逾65萬人次。

headline: 警方觀塘掃黃行動共拘捕14名內地女子
excerpt:
觀塘警區上月29日起，一連7日展開掃黃及移除宣傳賣淫標誌行動，打擊區內多個賣淫黑點，包括物華街、輔仁街和康寧道，並在觀塘拘捕共14名內地女子，年齡介乎25至53歲，當中11名女子已被控「為不道德目的而唆使他人」及「違反逗留條件」罪，其餘3名女子交由相關部門遣返內地。
 
警員同時巡查區內多個大廈，發現其中3座大廈的部分單位公開展示標誌，涉及宣傳或可合理被理解為宣傳賣淫，所有相關標誌已被警方檢取。
 
警方重申，任何人在公眾地方或在公眾可見的情況下，為不道德目的而唆使其他人，或者因不道德目的唆使其他人而在公眾地方遊蕩，即屬違法，最高可被判處監禁6個月及罰款一萬元。



編輯：陳樂謙
summary_zh: 警方在觀塘展開為期7日的掃黃行動，拘捕14名內地女子，其中11人被控違法，3人被遣返；同時檢取多個大廈內宣傳賣淫的標誌。

headline: 陳群獲任聯合國教科文組織教育助理總幹事　助推動教育體系轉型
excerpt:
聯合國教科文組織總幹事埃納尼宣布，聘任來自中國的陳群為負責教育事務的助理總幹事，是第二次由中國人擔任這個職位。

教科文組織的公告說，陳群是一名學者、物理學家及教育專家，擁有超過30年的學術與行政領導經驗。他曾任華東師範大學教授，並擔任高級管理職務。在國際層面，他長期參與教科文組織相關工作，曾於2012年至2017年擔任設立於華東師範大學的教科文組織教師教育教席主持人，自2021年起就任教科文組織教師教育中心理事會主席，致力於推動國際學術合作及國際教師培養。

公告說陳群將協助總幹事推動教育體系轉型，落實公平包容優質的教育，同時幫助全球學習者掌握最先進的技能，以造福人類、促進世界和平。



編輯：羅宇光
summary_zh: 中國學者陳群獲聯合國教科文組織聘任為教育助理總幹事，將協助推動全球教育體系轉型，是該職位第二次由中國人擔任。

headline: Polymarket removes wagers on U.S. service member rescue mission in Iran
excerpt: Rep. Seth Moulton, D-Mass., called the market "DISGUSTING" and said bettors were wagering on whether American troops would be saved.
full_text:
Polymarket removed a forum related to the rescue mission of U.S. military servicemembers amid political pressure, the latest sign of mounting scrutiny around prediction markets.

Rep. Seth Moulton, D-Mass., decried the Polymarket page that allowed users to bet on which day the U.S. would confirm the rescue of the two airmen after an American F-15E fighter jet was shot down over Iran. The lawmaker called the page "DISGUSTING" in an X post.

"They could be your neighbor, a friend, a family member," Moulton wrote on Friday. "And people are betting on whether or not they'll be saved."

In a response on X, Polymarket said: "We took this market down immediately as it does not meet our integrity standards."

"It should not have been posted, and we are investigating how this slipped through our internal safeguards," Polymarket wrote.

In a separate X post, Polymarket said it doesn't "make money or charge any fees on any geopolitical markets."

U.S. and Iranian military forces are searching for a missing American airman after its F-15E fighter jet was shot down over Iran on Friday. One crew member has been rescued, but another is not accounted for.

Moulton last month banned his staff from using prediction market platforms like Polymarket or Kalshi, a policy that his office believes is the first of its kind in Congress.

"Constituents that we serve should trust us to make decisions based on the right thing for do for our nation, not based on how bets might turn out," Moulton said Monday on CNBC's "Squawk Box."

Moulton also said on X that Donald Trump Jr., the son of President Donald Trump, "is an investor in this dystopian death market and may have access to intelligence that isn't public yet."

Requests for comment from Moulton and Trump Jr. weren't immediately returned to CNBC.

The Massachusetts lawmaker is part of a growing chorus of voices in Washington calling for stronger oversight of these betting platforms as interest swells.

A group of congressional Democrats introduced legislation late last month that would bar prediction markets from allowing wagers on elections, war and government actions, in addition to sports.

In February, six Democratic senators urged the Commodity Futures Trading Commission to clarify that it will prohibit any contracts related to an individual's death. These contracts "present dangerous national security risks," the lawmakers wrote.

The CFTC on Thursday announced lawsuits against three states over what it saw as efforts to circumvent the organization's sole regulatory authority over prediction markets.

The NFL has also asked prediction market operators to keep specific event contracts that the league deems "objectionable bets" off their platforms. The league outlined examples of event contracts that could be easily manipulated, inherently objectionable, related to officiating, and knowable in advance — and asked that operators refrain from offering such trades.

— CNBC's Dan Mangan, Azhar Sukri and Luke Fountain contributed to this report.

Disclosure: CNBC and Kalshi have a commercial relationship that includes customer acquisition and a minority investment.
summary_zh: 預測市場平台Polymarket因政治壓力下架了關於美國軍人在伊朗救援任務的賭盤，引發議員批評民眾對軍人生死進行賭博。

headline: 內地清明假期首日道路交通平穩有序　未接到嚴重交通擠塞報告
excerpt:
內地一連3日清明節假期的首日，主要高速公路交通流量明顯上升，部分地區進出城、景區墓區周邊道路交通流量較大。央視報道，截至昨日傍晚6時，除個別路段因交通大流量導致緩行外，全國道路交通總體平穩有序，未接到嚴重交通擠塞報告。

內地清明假期與港澳公眾假期重疊，港珠澳大橋珠海公路口岸迎來入境客流車流高峰。珠海邊檢總站港珠澳大橋邊檢站數據顯示，自前日以來，截至昨日下午4時，經口岸出入境人員超過19萬2千人次，車輛超過3萬2千輛。邊檢站預計口岸將於明日及後日迎來出境客流車流高峰，呼籲通關旅客及時關注口岸資訊，錯峰出行。



編輯：鄭皓璟
summary_zh: 內地清明假期首日道路交通平穩有序，全國未出現嚴重擠塞；港珠澳大橋口岸迎來入境高峰，已超19萬人次出入境。
